note: A rule is data, not code: each record names the evaluator that executes it against a Design. Relocating the rules into this spec changes no decision (proven by the parity tests).

rules.mechanism: the writer family's required targeting element must be present at the site (Cas NGG/TTTV PAM; CAST GTN PAM + fixed insertion distance; serine integrase attB/attP; bridge/seek central CT core; PE-installable), else the writer physically cannot engage the locus

provenance.note: v3.2 MC1 hard filter; per-family motifs in configs/target_sites.yaml

rules.note: v3.2 MC1 hard filter; per-family motifs in configs/target_sites.yaml
rules.scope: sequence-computable screen, not a guarantee of activity (relaxed-PAM engineered variants approximate)

rules.mechanism: bridge-RNA target/donor binding loops must not be mutually (or self-) complementary above threshold, or they recombine with each other (TBL-DBL / self cross-loop)

provenance.note: v3.1.5 fold_qc cross-loop screen (ViennaRNA fold optional)

rules.note: v3.1.5 fold_qc cross-loop screen (ViennaRNA fold optional)
rules.scope: crude antiparallel-complementarity screen; sequence-level, not a full co-fold simulation

rules.mechanism: cargo size (bp) must not exceed the delivery vehicle's packaging capacity
rules.scope: capacity is a hard packaging limit; efficiency near the limit is a separate soft penalty

rules.mechanism: an AAV cargo above a single capsid (~4.7 kb) requires split/dual AAV, which drops efficiency sharply

provenance.note: AAV packaging capacity (~4.7 kb); oversize cargo needs split/dual AAV

rules.note: AAV packaging capacity (~4.7 kb); oversize cargo needs split/dual AAV
rules.scope: directional efficiency flag, not a titre prediction

rules.mechanism: in a multi-edit plan, concurrent double-strand breaks at different loci can mis-join into a translocation; risk rises with cut count / cut probability / proximity, and is ~zero for DSB-free writers
rules.scope: interpretable pairwise screen, not a calibrated translocation-rate predictor

rules.mechanism: the writer's output form (DNA cargo / mRNA / RNP) must be carriable by the chosen vehicle (e.g. an RNP-only payload cannot ride a DNA-only AAV; an mRNA/RNP rides LNP or eVLP)
rules.scope: form compatibility is computable; precise tropism is not

rules.mechanism: if the goal forbids genomic integration, an integrating vehicle (e.g. lentivirus) is illegal
rules.scope: integration status is a documented vehicle property

rules.mechanism: vehicle-specific construct hazards (lentiviral internal poly(A), AAV inverted-repeat/homopolymer, recombinogenic direct repeats, GC extremes) lower titre/yield

provenance.note: AAV genome/sequence constraints; v3.2 MC2 delivery_constraints scan (labelled heuristic, per-check sources in configs/delivery_constraints.yaml)

rules.note: AAV genome/sequence constraints; v3.2 MC2 delivery_constraints scan (labelled heuristic, per-check sources in configs/delivery_constraints.yaml)
rules.scope: labeled heuristic, directional; not a titre predictor

rules.mechanism: AAV packaging efficiency / titre drops sharply as the cargo approaches the capsid limit (computable from cargo_bp vs vehicle capacity), even while still under capacity (v4.0 delivery-oracle refinement)
rules.scope: computable efficiency margin, directional; not a titre predictor

rules.mechanism: the MAGNITUDE of immunogenicity and the precise in-vivo tropism of a vehicle are not modeled
rules.scope: declared out of scope; never a hard reject dressed as physics

rules.mechanism: heritable human germline editing is out of scope for this somatic tool and broadly prohibited: a declared germline/heritable intent, a reproductive germline target cell, or an in-vivo edit of a germline-competent cell type is rejected

provenance.note: Heritable human germline genome editing is broadly prohibited / under moratorium: Lander et al. 2019 international call for a moratorium (Nature 567:165); US National Academies 2017 'Human Genome Editing: Science, Ethics, and Governance'; WHO Expert Advisory Committee framework (2021); US FDA is barred from considering heritable-germline clinical applications (Consolidated Appropriations Act rider); Council of Europe Oviedo Convention Art. 13. PEN-STACK is a SOMATIC tool and takes no position on germline editing beyond declaring it out of scope.

rules.note: Heritable human germline genome editing is broadly prohibited / under moratorium: Lander et al. 2019 international call for a moratorium (Nature 567:165); US National Academies 2017 'Human Genome Editing: Science, Ethics, and Governance'; WHO Expert Advisory Committee framework (2021); US FDA is barred from considering heritable-germline clinical applications (Consolidated Appropriations Act rider); Council of Europe Oviedo Convention Art. 13. PEN-STACK is a SOMATIC tool and takes no position on germline editing beyond declaring it out of scope.
rules.scope: a scope-of-use / regulatory legality bound, not a biosecurity-hazard signature and not an efficacy claim
